notable_pick_trades: Acquired No. 16 overall (2026 1st from IND via Sauce Gardner trade), No. 44 overall (2026 2nd from DAL via Quinnen Williams trade). Own three 2027 1sts (own + IND + higher of DAL's two). No 3rd-round pick (sent to PHI in 2024 Haason Reddick trade). Expected comp 4th (D.J. Reed loss) and comp 5th (Morgan Moses loss). Traded Jermaine Johnson to TEN for DT T'Vondre Sweat (pending 3/11).
roster_context: Coming off a catastrophic 3-14 season under first-year HC Aaron Glenn. The Jets executed a full-scale teardown at the 2025 trade deadline, shipping out franchise cornerstones Sauce Gardner and Quinnen Williams for a massive haul of draft capital (5 first-round picks over two drafts). Justin Fields was a disaster at QB and is expected to be cut. The defense set an NFL record with zero interceptions all season and ranked 31st in sacks (26). The offense ranked dead last (32nd) in passing. The only bright spots were an emerging young OL core (Fashanu, Membou, Tippmann), Breece Hall's 1,000-yard rushing season, and rookie TE Mason Taylor. The Jets have ~$90M in cap space, four picks in the top 44, and massive roster holes on both sides of the ball — a true ground-up rebuild.

needs.explanation: The OL core of Fashanu-Tippmann-Membou is excellent and all under contract. However, LG is wide open with both John Simpson and Alijah Vera-Tucker hitting free agency. The Jets are reportedly willing to let both test the market. Center Josh Myers was extended but is adequate, not a difference-maker. The Jets may target Tyler Linderbaum in FA for center and could draft a LG (Vega Ioane at No. 16 has been discussed). One starting guard spot needs to be filled.
needs.current_starter: Josh Myers (C, extended), LG vacant (Simpson/AVT both UFAs)
needs.depth: Joe Tippmann provides versatility (can play C or G). Max Mitchell (UFA). Need at least one starting guard.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 if top IOL available, or free agency

needs.explanation: After trading Sauce Gardner midseason, the Jets' cornerback room is thin. Brandon Stephens emerged as CB1 and is under contract (signed 2025 FA deal). Rookie AZ Thomas showed flashes before going on IR but is still a project. Isaiah Oliver and Ja'Sir Taylor are UFAs. The Jets need a true CB2 opposite Stephens and depth throughout the secondary. The zero-interception season underscores how badly the secondary needs an overhaul.
needs.current_starter: Brandon Stephens (CB1), AZ Thomas (CB2, returning from IR)
needs.depth: Jarvis Brownlee, Michael Carter II — very thin after top two. Multiple CBs are UFAs.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-3 or free agency target (Tariq Woolen linked)

needs.explanation: The Jets ranked 31st in sacks with just 26 in 2025. Will McDonald IV led the team with only 8 sacks. Jermaine Johnson was traded to the Titans, leaving an enormous void opposite McDonald. The pass rush was historically bad and is the most likely target at No. 2 overall (Arvell Reese or David Bailey are the frontrunners). This is Glenn's top defensive priority as he takes over playcalling duties.
needs.depth: Extremely thin. McDonald is the only viable edge rusher. Micheal Clemons (UFA) is gone. Need at least two new bodies.

needs.explanation: The Jets cycled through Justin Fields (2-7, benched), Tyrod Taylor (4 starts, injured), and UDFA Brady Cook in 2025. Fields is expected to be released, absorbing $22M in dead cap. Taylor is a UFA at age 37. The 2026 passing offense ranked dead last in the NFL. New OC Frank Reich needs a real QB. The weak 2026 draft class (only Mendoza at No. 1 is a consensus 1st-rounder) means the Jets will likely pursue a veteran bridge QB in FA while potentially drafting Ty Simpson at No. 16 or waiting for the deeper 2027 class with their three first-rounders.
needs.current_starter: None (Fields expected cut; Taylor UFA; Cook only QB under contract)
needs.depth: Brady Cook (UDFA), Hendon Hooker, Bailey Zappe (reserve/future) — no viable starter on roster
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1 (No. 16) if a QB like Ty Simpson is there, plus veteran FA bridge

needs.explanation: Garrett Wilson is elite but missed the final 10 games with a knee injury in 2025, and still led the team with only 395 receiving yards. The Jets ranked 32nd in passing offense. No other WR had more than 36 catches. John Metchie III and Adonai Mitchell (acquired from IND) are under team control but unproven as consistent contributors. The WR room behind Wilson is among the NFL's worst. New OC Frank Reich's system will be more pass-forward, demanding better receiving talent.
needs.current_starter: Garrett Wilson (WR1), Adonai Mitchell (WR2), John Metchie III (RFA)
needs.depth: Isaiah Williams (returner/gadget), Metchie, Mitchell — all unproven. No reliable WR2.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (No. 16 for Makai Lemon, Denzel Boston, or Carnell Tate)

needs.explanation: Nick Folk (the NFL's most accurate kicker for 3 straight seasons) departed to the Falcons on a 2-year deal. The Jets now have no established kicker on the roster. This is a real need that must be addressed before Week 1. Escalates from a depth/afterthought position to a genuine offseason priority.

Quincy Williams was the emotional leader of the defense but had a terrible 2025 season, was benched at times, and is expected to sign elsewhere as a UFA (linked to Titans/Saleh). Jamien Sherwood is under contract on a 3-year, $45M extension but struggled badly in coverage (119.7 passer rating against). Rookie Kiko Mauigoa showed promise but was limited. The Jets need a coverage-capable LB to pair with Sherwood. Alex Anzalone (Glenn's former player in DET) is a top FA target.
needs.depth: Kiko Mauigoa (2nd-year), Mykal Walker (UFA). Quincy Williams (UFA, expected to leave).
needs.draft_range_target: Round 2-4, plus FA signing (Anzalone, Devin Lloyd linked)

needs.explanation: The Jets' defense did not record a single interception in 2025 — an NFL record for futility. Safety Andre Cisco is a UFA, and Tony Adams is also a UFA. Rookie Malachi Moore showed promise (85 tackles) but is still developing. The Jets need two starting-caliber safeties. Multiple mock drafters have linked Caleb Downs (Ohio State) to the Jets at No. 2. The team is reportedly considering Bryan Cook, Nick Cross, and Minkah Fitzpatrick (trade) in free agency.
needs.depth: Andre Cisco (UFA), Tony Adams (UFA) — both expected to leave. Moore is the only safety under contract.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (No. 2 if Downs, or Round 2 at No. 33-44)

needs.explanation: Jelani Woods re-signed but contract undisclosed and production was minimal (4 games, 1 catch). Jeremy Ruckert is the incumbent. Frank Reich's offense values TE heavily. This position has been minimally addressed and remains a need in rounds 3-5.

After trading Quinnen Williams at the deadline, the Jets gutted their interior D-line. The acquisition of T'Vondre Sweat from Tennessee helps significantly as a nose tackle/run stuffer, and Jowon Briggs flashed in 2025 (4 sacks as an interior rusher). Harrison Phillips is a solid veteran. Mazi Smith (acquired from DAL) is a reclamation project. The group is functional but lacks the game-wrecking interior pass rush that Williams provided. Long-term, they need more investment.
needs.current_starter: T'Vondre Sweat (NT), Jowon Briggs (3-tech), Harrison Phillips
needs.depth: Mazi Smith, Khalen Saunders (UFA), Jay Tufele (UFA). Sweat trade and Briggs help but need another pass-rushing DT.
needs.draft_range_target: Round 1-2 (No. 16 for Peter Woods or Kayden McDonald)

scheme_fit_notes: Aaron Glenn is taking over defensive playcalling in 2026 with new DC Brian Duker. Glenn prefers a 3-4 base that values versatile EDGE/LB hybrids (hence Arvell Reese fits perfectly — a college off-ball LB transitioning to EDGE). The defense will feature T'Vondre Sweat as a true nose tackle with space-eaters inside and aggressive edge rushers outside. Glenn's background as a former DB coach means he values instinctive, ball-hawking safeties (Caleb Downs profile). On offense, new OC Frank Reich runs a more traditional, pass-forward system — a stark departure from the mobility-focused scheme that catered to Fields. The Jets need a pocket passer or dual-threat who can operate within structure. Reich's offense elevates WR and TE usage, making a true WR2 essential.
